events.text: Jeffrey Garcia, 75-year-old Hispanic male, Never smoker. Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9 of the Prostate. Clinical staging: T3bN1M1b, AJCC Stage IV. ICD-10: C61. Stage IV castration-resistant prostate cancer with bone metastases.
Bone scan performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Initiated first-line ADT with ARPI: Leuprolide 22.5 mg IM q3mo (ADT) + Enzalutamide 160 mg daily. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for follow-up on first-line ADT with ARPI.
Clinical visit for follow-up on first-line ADT with ARPI.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging on first-line ADT with ARPI.
Initiated first-line chemotherapy for CRPC: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Prednisone 5 mg BID. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated first-line chemotherapy for CRPC: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Prednisone 5 mg BID. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 1 neuropathy. Dose reduction of neurotoxic agent by 25%. Gabapentin 300 mg TID started.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated first-line chemotherapy for CRPC: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Prednisone 5 mg BID. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 1 hot flashes. Venlafaxine 37.5 mg daily started. Counseled on lifestyle modifications.
Initiated first-line chemotherapy for CRPC: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Prednisone 5 mg BID. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 1 hot flashes. Venlafaxine 37.5 mg daily started. Counseled on lifestyle modifications.
Bone scan performed for restaging after first-line chemotherapy for CRPC.
Clinical visit for results review after first-line chemotherapy for CRPC.
Completed palliative bone radiation: Palliative radiation 30 Gy in 10 fractions to L3 vertebral body. Treatment delivered as planned.
Clinical visit for post-radiation assessment.
Initiated second-line PARP inhibitor for BRCA2-mutant CRPC: Olaparib 300 mg BID. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for follow-up on second-line PARP inhibitor for BRCA2-mutant CRPC.
Clinical visit for follow-up on second-line PARP inhibitor for BRCA2-mutant CRPC.
PSMA PET/CT performed for restaging on second-line PARP inhibitor for BRCA2-mutant CRPC.
Bone scan performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Bone scan performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
PSMA PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.

documents.text:
BONE SCAN REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Garcia
MRN: patient_56e050b78b97
Date of Study: 2022-10-08

CLINICAL INDICATION: Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Whole body bone scintigraphy performed following IV injection of 25 mCi Tc-99m MDP.

FINDINGS:
Multiple foci of increased radiotracer uptake in the axial and appendicular skeleton consistent with widespread osseous metastatic disease. Approximately 11 lesions identified.

IMPRESSION:
1. Widespread osseous metastatic disease.
2. Pelvic lymphadenopathy consistent with nodal metastases.

Electronically signed by: Jennifer Walsh, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Garcia
MRN: patient_56e050b78b97
Specimen ID: S2022-53747
Date of Procedure: 2022-10-12
Date of Report: 2022-10-17

SPECIMEN: Prostate, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 6.2 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PSA: Positive
NKX3.1: Positive
AR: Positive
ERG: Positive (TMPRSS2-ERG fusion likely)

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Jeffrey Garcia - Prostate" is a firm tan tissue fragment measuring 6.2 x 4.3 x 3.1 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A10.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of nests of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

Electronically signed by: Michael Torres, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Jeffrey Garcia
MRN: patient_56e050b78b97
Date: 2022-10-19
Provider: Dr. Richard Okafor

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Garcia is a 75-year-old male who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed acinar adenocarcinoma, gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate, IV. Patient was referred by primary care physician after abnormal imaging. Stage IV castration-resistant prostate cancer with bone metastases. Pathology confirmed acinar adenocarcinoma, gleason 4+5=9. Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports palpable mass noted by patient.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- peripheral vascular disease
- type 2 diabetes mellitus

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- No prior surgeries

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Former smoker (quit 10 years ago)
- Alcohol: No alcohol use
- Occupation: Accountant
- Lives with partner

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Sister with ovarian cancer at age 55.

ALLERGIES: Iodine contrast (anaphylaxis)

MEDICATIONS:
- Metformin 1000 mg BID

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: decreased appetite, joint pain, dizziness, intermittent headaches. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 138/83, HR 77, RR 15, Temp 97.8F, SpO2 99% on RA, Wt 62.0 kg
General: Alert, oriented, well-appearing, in no acute distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis or clubbing.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 5.6, Hgb 14.4, Plt 248, Cr 1.02

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
75-year-old male with IV acinar adenocarcinoma, gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate. initial oncology consultation.

1. Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate, Stage IV
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend first-line ADT with ARPI
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - PICC line to be placed prior to treatment
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Helen Park, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Garcia
MRN: patient_56e050b78b97
Date of Report: 2022-10-26
Specimen: Prostate (FNA cell block)
Diagnosis: Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9
Panel: FoundationOne CDx (648 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 5.7 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  BRCA2 p.E1493fs (c.4478delA) - Frameshift (Exon 11) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.R273C - Missense (Exon 8)
  AR Amplification - Amplification

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  ATM p.S1893R - VUS
  CDK12 p.Q1142H - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - BRCA2 pathogenic variant: FDA-approved PARP inhibitor olaparib indicated for HRR-mutated mCRPC.
  - Consider platinum-based chemotherapy.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 696x coverage.
Tumor content: 61%.

Electronically signed by: Katherine Liu, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Urology

Patient: Jeffrey Garcia
MRN: patient_56e050b78b97
Date: 2022-12-02
Provider: Dr. Helen Park

CHIEF COMPLAINT: follow-up on first-line ADT with ARPI

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Garcia is a 75-year-old male with IV acinar adenocarcinoma, gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate who presents for follow-up on first-line adt with arpi. Patient reports feeling well overall. Appetite has improved.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: abdominal bloating, dizziness, decreased appetite, mild cough. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 132/67, HR 67, RR 19, Temp 97.5F, SpO2 95% on RA, Wt 72.8 kg
General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 4.0, Hgb 12.8, Plt 297, Cr 0.94

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
75-year-old male with IV acinar adenocarcinoma, gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate. follow-up on first-line ADT with ARPI.

1. Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9 - follow-up on first-line ADT with ARPI
   - Restaging imaging to be scheduled
   - Tumor markers trending favorably
2. Symptom management
   - Neuropathy being monitored
3. Follow-up in 3 weeks

Electronically signed by: Priya Sharma, MD
Urology

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Garcia
MRN: patient_56e050b78b97
Date of Study: 2023-01-27

CLINICAL INDICATION: Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate. restaging on first-line ADT with ARPI.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: Enlarged prostate measuring 5.0 cm. Bilateral pelvic lymphadenopathy, largest node 2.8 cm.

BONES: Multiple lytic osseous lesions involving pelvis and bilateral iliac bones consistent with osseous metastatic disease.

IMPRESSION:
1. Widespread osseous metastatic disease.
2. Pelvic lymphadenopathy consistent with nodal metastases.

Electronically signed by: Sophia Martinez, MD
Department of Radiology

BONE SCAN REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Garcia
MRN: patient_56e050b78b97
Date of Study: 2024-04-20

CLINICAL INDICATION: Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Comparison made to prior study from approximately 2 months ago.

TECHNIQUE: Whole body bone scintigraphy performed following IV injection of 25 mCi Tc-99m MDP.

FINDINGS:
Multiple foci of increased radiotracer uptake in the pelvis and lumbar spine consistent with widespread osseous metastatic disease. Approximately 11 lesions identified.

IMPRESSION:
1. Widespread osseous metastatic disease.
2. Pelvic lymphadenopathy consistent with nodal metastases.
3. Overall assessment: Partial response per RECIST 1.1.

PSMA PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Garcia
MRN: patient_56e050b78b97
Date of Study: 2024-09-29

CLINICAL INDICATION: Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Comparison made to prior study from approximately 3 months ago.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: Enlarged prostate measuring 5.3 cm. Bilateral pelvic lymphadenopathy, largest node 2.2 cm.

BONES: Multiple mixed lytic-sclerotic osseous lesions involving thoracic and lumbar spine consistent with osseous metastatic disease.

IMPRESSION:
1. Widespread osseous metastatic disease.
2. Pelvic lymphadenopathy consistent with nodal metastases.
3. Overall assessment: Stable disease per RECIST 1.1.

Electronically signed by: Robert Chang, MD
Department of Radiology

scenario_blurb: Stage IV castration-resistant prostate cancer with bone metastases
